Lovable, the Stockholm vibe-coding startup, signed a multiyear deal with Google Cloud involving a fivefold increase in its usage footprint, including expanded access to Anthropic's Claude and Google's Gemini models.
Trump told reporters he had called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu 'crazy' during a phone call, even as both men said their rapport remains solid.

On Monday night alone, at least 22 people were killed in combined missile and drone strikes across Ukraine, while on Wednesday seven people were killed after a drone hit a passenger bus in the Russia-controlled Donetsk region.

The US and Bahraini forces intercepted missiles aimed at Bahrain, home to the US Navy's 5th Fleet, with Bahrain's Defense Ministry saying its military destroyed three missiles and a number of drones fired by Iran.

Iran's IRGC said it attacked 'enemy bases in the region' with 'aerospace missiles', though the US military said none of the seven fired missiles reached their targets in Kuwait or Bahrain.
Trump told reporters on Friday that 'the situation with Iran seems to be going quite well' and that Iranians still have 21% to 22% of their missiles.

Alphabet said it plans to sell $85 billion in stock, including a $10 billion investment by Berkshire Hathaway, with Google revising its capital expenditure forecast for the year to between $180 billion and $190 billion.
The Nasdaq tumbled 4% as shares in chip and memory groups sank, with neocloud stocks CoreWeave and Nebius among those hammered in a broader tech selloff on Friday.
